title: We That Were Friends
content:
We that were friends to-night have found
    A sudden fear, a secret flame:
    I am on fire with that soft sound
    You make, in uttering my name.

    Forgive a young and boastful man
    Whom dreams delight and passions please,
    And love me as great women can
    Who have no children at their knees.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Elroy Flecker, titled "We That Were Friends", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
